extraction.one_line_summary: WELLENS Tim won a three-up sprint at Hulst by exploiting a tactical rest in HIRSCHI's slipstream before throwing on the uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: DE PLUS Laurens repeatedly attacked on climbs to fragment the group, showing Jumbo-Visma's willingness to use him aggressively.
GARCÍA CORTINA Iván bridged to leaders but was deliberately dropped by the trio before the finish to eliminate sprint threat.
WELLENS used sprinting experience to tuck into slipstream on drag before final acceleration, beating younger HIRSCHI on throw.
No team controlled the race despite weather and terrain favoring breakaways, creating chaotic multi-attack selection race.

rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: positive: Initiated multiple successful splits on climbs showing climbing punch superior to teammates' expectations for him
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: Won from break and takes race lead, demonstrated tactical maturity exploiting uphill finish drag positioning
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: positive: At 20 years old matched DE PLUS and WELLENS on repeated climbs, only lost sprint by centimeters
rider_signals.GARCÍA CORTINA Iván: neutral: Strong enough to bridge gap alone but deliberately isolated and dropped as sprint threat before finale
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Expected to be strong in these conditions but was dropped when DE PLUS split the race
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: Reached for rain cape early suggesting he knew course was too hard, abandoned fight for stage
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: negative: Crashed in poor conditions, suggesting discomfort on technical wet parcours
